name: Sure.jell® Apricot-Pineapple Jam
recipeIngredient: 5 cups prepared fruit (about 2-1/2 pounds fully ripe apricots and 1-1/2 fully ripe medium pineapples)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Place the jar on a rack in the boiling water and let it sit for 10 minutes.
2. Put flat lids in a saucepan filled with water and bring to a boil. Remove from heat and let jars and lids stand in hot water until ready to fill.
3. Drain well before filling. Pit apricots; do not peel. Finely chop or grind the fruit.
4. Measure 3 1/2 cups into a 6- or 8-quart saucepot.
5. Pare and core pineapples; finely chop or grind. Measure 1 1/2 cups into the saucepot.
6. Add any fruit protector, if desired.
7. In a separate bowl, measure sugar (scrape excess with a spatula to level).
8. Stir pectin into the fruit in the saucepot.
9. Add margarine.
10. Bring mixture to full rolling boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
11. Quickly stir in all sugar. Return to full rolling boil and boil exactly 1 minute, stirring constantly.
12. Remove from heat. Use a metal spoon to skim off any foam.
13. Ladle quickly into prepared jars, filling to within 1/8 inch of the top.
14. Wipe jar rims and threads. Cover with two-piece lids.
15. Screw bands tightly.
16. Invert jars for 5 minutes, then turn upright. After jars are cool, check seals. Alternatively, follow the water bath method recommended by USDA.
Note: Jam sets slowly; allow 1 week.
